Intrinsic Fast-Path

The intrinsic fast-path answers queries on intrinsic columns entirely from the file-level intrinsic section — without reading any block payloads. Zero block I/O.

Open Interactive Demo
Explore the zero-I/O intrinsic fast-path with animated visualization
→

How It Works

When blockpack writes a file, it stores every intrinsic column value in a dedicated intrinsic section in the file metadata. Each value is paired with a BlockRef — a pointer to the exact block and row where that span lives. The values are sorted, enabling O(log N) binary search.

At query time, if the compiler detects that every predicate in the query targets an intrinsic column, it sets intrinsicOnly=true and the executor skips the entire block-pruning pipeline. Instead, it performs a binary search directly on the intrinsic section data. The result is a set of BlockRefs pointing to matching spans — no block payloads are read at all.

For queries that only need the intrinsic column values themselves (tag discovery, metrics aggregation), the answer is fully contained in the intrinsic section. For queries that need full span data, the BlockRefs identify the minimal set of blocks to read.

Which Columns Are Intrinsic

ColumnTypeDescription
trace:idBytes128-bit trace identifier
span:idBytes64-bit span identifier
span:parent_idBytes64-bit parent span identifier
span:nameStringSpan operation name
span:durationUint64Span duration in nanoseconds
span:kindIntSpan kind (client, server, producer, consumer, internal)
span:startUint64Span start timestamp in nanoseconds
span:statusIntSpan status code (OK, Error, Unset)
span:status_messageStringSpan status message
resource.service.nameStringService name from resource attributes

When This Path Is Used

The intrinsic fast-path activates when all predicates in a query target intrinsic columns. Examples:

  • { span:duration > 5ms } — single intrinsic predicate
  • { span:kind = server && span:duration > 10ms } — multiple intrinsic predicates
  • { resource.service.name = "frontend" } — service name is intrinsic

If any predicate targets a non-intrinsic column (e.g., span.http.status_code), the full block-pruning pipeline is used instead.

Key Advantage

Zero block I/O, O(log N) lookup. The intrinsic section is stored in the file metadata and read once at file open. Queries on intrinsic columns never touch block payloads, making them orders of magnitude faster than the full pruning pipeline.
